campbell.title: Campbell City Council — February 3, 2026
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to approve a $4,000 donation from Water Tower Kitchen to help pay for youth recreation scholarships. They also discussed plans to build a bicycle and pedestrian bridge over Highway 17 at Hamilton Avenue, including approving a completed feasibility study and submitting a grant application to help fund the project.
keyTopics: Water Tower Kitchen donates $4,000 to youth scholarship fund
Hamilton Avenue bicycle bridge over Highway 17 moving forward
City applying for grant to help pay for bike/pedestrian overcrossing
Extension of interim ordinance number 2326

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 18, 2026
saratoga.summary: The city council held a public meeting on March 18, 2026 where residents had a chance to speak about a proposed new rule or policy. After hearing from the public, the council decided to move forward quickly by skipping the usual first step of reviewing the proposed ordinance and instead putting it directly on the agenda for a final vote at their next meeting.
keyTopics: Public hearing held to gather community input
New ordinance being fast-tracked for adoption
Final vote scheduled for next regular council meeting
First reading waived to expedite the process

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — April 7, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council met on April 7, 2026, to approve meeting minutes from various March sessions and authorize the Town Manager to sign a construction contract with QLM, Inc. The agenda was mostly administrative housekeeping from previous meetings.
keyTopics: Approved multiple sets of meeting minutes from March 2026
Authorized construction contract with QLM, Inc.
Routine administrative and procedural approvals

san-jose.title: San José City Council — April 21, 2026
san-jose.summary: San José City Council met to approve several infrastructure and housing-related agreements. The council discussed updating the airport's baggage system contract with Vanderlande Industries, protecting affordable housing when subsidies end, hiring a consultant for storm sewer system planning, and moving forward with a FEMA family camp project.
keyTopics: Airport baggage handling system contract renewal
Affordable housing protections if funding runs out
Storm sewer system master planning
FEMA San José Family Camp project
Approval of committee meeting minutes

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — April 14, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council met to discuss the city's spending plans for the next fiscal year, including what projects and improvements will get funding. The council also officially recognized Teen Month and National Volunteer Week to honor young people and volunteers in the community.
keyTopics: Budget and funding decisions for city construction and improvement projects in 2026-27
Teen Month proclamation recognizing young people in Mountain View
National Volunteer Week proclamation honoring community volunteers

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — April 7, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale City Council held their regular meeting on April 7, 2026, where they opened with the Pledge of Allegiance and recognized two important community observances: Arab-American Heritage Month and Fair Housing Month. These recognitions highlight the city's commitment to celebrating its diverse residents and ensuring equal housing opportunities for everyone.
keyTopics: Arab-American Heritage Month recognition
Fair Housing Month observance
Community diversity celebrations
Housing rights and equality initiatives

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — April 13, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council held a workshop to help residents learn about and provide feedback on OpenGov, a new tool the city is using to show the public where tax dollars are being spent. The council also discussed rules requiring people who lobby the city on specific issues to register and report their activities.
keyTopics: OpenGov financial transparency tool demonstration and community feedback
City budget overview and spending priorities
Lobbyist registration and reporting requirements under municipal code
Public engagement on how city tax dollars are allocated

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 16, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The City Council meeting on April 16, 2026 included important legal notices for residents. If you disagree with a city decision and want to sue or challenge it in court, you have 90 days from when the decision is final to file your lawsuit.
keyTopics: 90-day deadline to file lawsuits against city decisions
Legal challenge procedures for residents
California Code of Civil Procedure requirements

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — April 7, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council met to review and potentially approve changes to the city's long-term housing and community development plans for 2025-2026. The council also discussed updating rules about how vacant council seats are filled and reviewed meeting schedules and requests from individual council members.
keyTopics: Changes to the city's 5-year housing and community development plan
New requests from council members for future agenda items
Update to the council vacancy ordinance (how to fill empty council seats)
Approval of previous meeting minutes

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — April 9, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council held a public hearing about a building at 405 Kipling Street in downtown. The owner wants to officially designate it as a historic building and get permission to add 5,000 square feet of new floor space as part of renovating and strengthening the structure for earthquake safety.
keyTopics: 405 Kipling Street historic building designation upgrade
5,000 square feet of bonus floor area for renovations
Seismic safety improvements to existing structure
Historic Resources Board meeting schedule updates
Downtown commercial zone development incentives
